channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Maple Leafs goaltender Stolarz exits early with leg injury against Capitals
item.description: Toronto Maple Leafs goaltender Anthony Stolarz left the game against the Washington Capitals early in the first period on April 8, 2026, after sustaining a lower-body injury. The incident occurred whe...

item.title: U.S. Stock Indexes Mixed as Investors Gauge Fed Rate Outlook and Earnings
item.description: On April 7, 2026, U.S. stock markets showed mixed performance as investors balanced optimism about corporate earnings with caution around future Federal Reserve policy decisions. The Dow Jones Industr...

item.title: Australia responds to Middle East conflict as Albanese seeks fuel security
item.description: The political landscape in Australia has been significantly shaped by the ongoing tensions in the Middle East. As the ceasefire between the US and Iran remains fragile, Australian Prime Minister Antho...

item.title: House Committee Orders NBI Director to Testify on Threat Allegations Against VP Sara Duterte
item.description: The House Committee on Justice has issued a subpoena to National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Atty. Melvin A. Matibag in connection with the ongoing impeachment hearings against Vice Preside...

item.title: Temporary Appointment of Sanitation Workers as BLOs in Hyderabad Due to Staff Shortage
item.description: In Hyderabad, residents of the old city were surprised during a pre-SIR (Summary Revision) mapping exercise when sanitation workers from the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation were appointed as b...

item.title: Farrer by-election shows major shifts in voter support for Coalition and One Nation
item.description: The upcoming Farrer by-election, triggered by Sussan Ley's resignation, could mark a significant shake-up in Australian politics. Polling data from RedBridge/Accent reveals a sharp rise for One Nation...

item.title: Unilorin VC Prof. Egbewole Calls for Unity, Compassion as Christians Celebrate Easter
item.description: The Vice Chancellor of University of Ilorin, Prof. Wahab Olasupo Egbewole, don extend im warm Easter greetings to all Christians wey dey inside the university community and across the whole country. A...

item.title: Rethinking Sustainability: Filipino Concepts for Regeneration in Business
item.description: In a recent dialogue led by the PHINMA-DLSU Center for Business and Society, experts explored the concept of regenerative sustainability in the context of Filipino business practices. The discussion r...

item.title: Study finds recent heatwaves created non-survivable conditions for older people using new physiological model
item.description: A new study led by Australian National University climate scientist Professor Sarah Perkins-Kirkpatrick has revealed that extreme heatwaves are already producing conditions too dangerous for human sur...

item.title: Rising fuel prices force more Philippine fishers to halt operations, threatening supply and food security
item.description: Surging fuel prices are pushing many Filipino fishers to stop their operations, putting the country's fishing industry at serious risk. According to industry leaders, fishing boats are now operating a...

item.title: Senate Blue Ribbon Committee defers flood control projects hearing as it awaits Chavit Singson's affidavit
item.description: The Senate Blue Ribbon Committee, chaired by Senator Panfilo Lacson, has decided to postpone its scheduled public hearing on April 14 regarding alleged anomalous flood control projects in the country....

item.title: Tension between Ruto and Gachagua spills over during Ol Kalou MP’s requiem mass
item.description: The requiem mass of the late Ol Kalou MP, David Njuguna Kiaraho, held at Captain grounds in Nyandarua County, saw a bitter clash between President William Ruto and Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua. T...

item.title: Over 20,000 students face repayment of maintenance loans due to course eligibility errors
item.description: More than 20,000 students enrolled on weekend university courses have been informed that their maintenance loans and, in some cases, childcare grants were incorrectly awarded. Letters from the Student...

item.title: US Coast Guard Investigates Disappearance of Michigan Woman in the Bahamas
item.description: The US Coast Guard has launched a criminal investigation into the disappearance of Lynette Hooker, a 55-year-old woman from Michigan, who fell off a dinghy while boating in the Bahamas. According to h...

item.title: Centre challenges Tamil Nadu law on appointment of priests, calling it a religious matter
item.description: The Centre has criticised a Tamil Nadu law that gives the state control over appointing priests (archakas) in temples, arguing that it undermines the religious autonomy of communities. Solicitor Gener...

item.title: Heavy rains cause fatalities and injuries in Punjab and KP; multiple accidents reported
item.description: Over the past 24 hours, heavy rains and thunderstorms across Punjab and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa have resulted in tragic loss of life and numerous injuries. According to rescue and district officials, at le...

item.title: Johannesburg Residents Struggle with Debt as Disconnection Notices Issued
item.description: Johannesburg is grappling with a growing debt crisis, as residents are receiving pre-termination notices for outstanding electricity and water bills. The city is implementing strict protocols for non-...

item.title: Zelenskyy Accuses US of Ignoring Russian-Iranian Collaboration on Attacks in the Middle East
item.description: In an interview with Alastair Campbell on The Rest is Politics podcast, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy criticised the US for failing to acknowledge compelling evidence that Russia has been as...

item.title: Kenya's Mobile Subscriptions Hit 51.4 Million, Focus Now Shifts to Usage and Engagement
item.description: Kenya's mobile subscriptions have experienced a significant rise, increasing by 9 million to a total of 51.36 million by December 2025, as reported by the Communications Authority of Kenya. This surge...

item.title: Senator Godfrey Osotsi admitted to hospital following attack by unknown assailants
item.description: Senator Godfrey Osotsi of Vihiga County was admitted to hospital after sustaining injuries during a violent attack that occurred late Wednesday night. Reports indicate that the legislator was assaulte...
